expectations.text: It explicitly mentions the AnyIO vs `pytest-asyncio` auto-mode conflict.
expectations.evidence: Line 1: 'putting both plugins in auto mode is wrong' and line 98: 'Don't set both anyio_mode = "auto" and asyncio_mode = "auto".'

expectations.text: It provides a concrete `anyio_backend` fixture or equivalent configuration.
expectations.evidence: Lines 41-43: '@pytest.fixture(params=_BACKENDS) def anyio_backend(request): return request.param' with full parametrized backend list.

expectations.text: It includes Trio and plain asyncio in the serious portability matrix.
It treats `asyncio+uvloop` as an additional runtime axis rather than a substitute for Trio coverage.
expectations.evidence: Lines 35-38: uvloop is conditionally appended to _BACKENDS list only if supported. Line 94: 'uvloop: it is not a third async model. It is only an asyncio runtime variant.'

expectations.text: It explicitly states that a higher-scope async fixture needs a matching higher-scope `anyio_backend` fixture.
expectations.evidence: Line 57: 'For the module-scoped TCP listener, the fixture scope must match anyio_backend scope in that module' with code showing scope="module" anyio_backend fixture at lines 66-76.

expectations.text: It explicitly mentions that AnyIO's test runner keeps async fixtures and tests in the same task, so `contextvars` can leak within that runner.
expectations.evidence: Line 91: 'AnyIO runs async fixtures/tests in the same task within a runner; with a module-scoped async fixture, contextvar mutations can leak across tests in that module.'

expectations.text: It gates any uvloop test axis to supported CPython/POSIX runtimes instead of enabling it unconditionally on Windows or PyPy.
expectations.evidence: Lines 26-27: '_supports_uvloop() checks sys.implementation.name == "cpython" and sys.platform != "win32"'. Lines 92-93: 'Windows: do not try uvloop... PyPy: do not try uvloop'
